subject: Geography
title: Traffic and Gridlock
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Traffic and Gridlock.

hook.text: Drivers in Bogotá spend an average of 191 hours per year stuck in traffic.

buildup.text: Cars flood megacities faster than roads can be built to hold them.

discovery.text: Bogotá's TransMilenio bus system moves 2.4 million people daily through dedicated bus lanes.

twist.text: Adding more roads often makes traffic worse—a paradox called 'induced demand.'

climax.text: The only proven way to reduce congestion is giving people alternatives to driving.

punchline.text: Building more roads is like loosening your belt to lose weight.

quiz.question: What is 'induced demand' in traffic planning?
options: Building more roads attracts more cars, worsening congestion
Demand for roads decreases over time
